blocks.content: Instrumenting a single service is straightforward. But in a real environment, you likely have dozens or hundreds of services, and new ones deploy regularly. Without automation, your observability coverage develops gaps.

Ongoing instrumentation solves this by automatically capturing new services as they appear. When enabled for a namespace, any new deployment is detected by Alloy and instrumented with your configured settings—no manual intervention required.

With ongoing instrumentation, you can:

- **Maintain complete coverage** — No services slip through unmonitored
- **Eliminate manual steps** — New deployments are handled automatically
- **Apply consistent settings** — All services in a namespace share the same configuration
- **Focus on insights** — Spend time analyzing data, not configuring instrumentation

This is how teams with hundreds of microservices maintain observability. Set it once, and your coverage scales with your environment.

In the next milestone, you'll navigate to the Manage tab where you can access namespace settings.

---

### More to explore (optional)

At this point in your journey, you can explore the following paths:

- [Manage instrumentation and settings](https://grafana.com/docs/grafana-cloud/get-started/inst-hub-setup/#manage-instrumentation-and-settings)
- [Application Observability overview](https://grafana.com/docs/grafana-cloud/monitor-applications/application-observability/)
